codeReview.summary: The feature correctly serializes/deserializes most leads filter state to URL params and sanitizes invalid values, but it has a back/forward regression: navigating to an empty query string does not re-apply default filter state, so UI state can diverge from URL.

issues.description: URL→state sync is gated by `if (currentStr && currentStr !== lastParamsRef.current)`, which skips parsing when `currentStr` is empty. After filters are set and the user navigates back to `/` (no query params), the effect returns without resetting filters/page to defaults. This violates expected back/forward URL-state behavior and leaves stale filter state while URL is empty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission validation contract for this feature does not explicitly cover the back/forward behavior required in the feature definition, which allowed a URL/history sync bug to pass implementation verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (url-state-persistence expectedBehavior) includes 'Browser back/forward preserves filter state', while validation-contract.md only defines VAL-INFRA-011 (URL reflects filter state) and VAL-INFRA-012 (params pre-apply on load). The gap is visible in src/App.tsx:338 where empty-query back navigation is not handled.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit 3e1f5d0 diff, transcript skeleton, and shared-state docs. Result: fail due to a blocking URL/history synchronization bug when navigating back to an empty query string; all other requested URL param mappings are present.
